adds.rationale: Upgrade the robotics exposure to a cleaner pure-play as the humanoid commercial breakout accelerates; ROBO's tiered structure (50% industrials, 37% tech, ~$3.7B AUM, dedicated since 2016) gives broader industrial-automation breadth than BOTZ's NVDA-top-weighted, AI-heavy mix.

news_evidence.summary: Humanoid robots have moved from proof-of-concept into mainstream adoption as Amazon, DHL, and UPS deploy large fleets to address labor constraints; ROBO is the longest-running dedicated US robotics fund and is up roughly 66% over the past year.

Figure AI reached a $39B valuation after raising $1B, with Figure 02 piloting at BMW; Tesla announced its third-generation Optimus debuts mid-2026 with mass production targeted for Q3 - a structural commercial inflection for the robotics wave.

removes.rationale: BOTZ just hit a 52-week high with NVIDIA as its top holding, so it largely double-counts the AI exposure already held directly through NVDA/GOOGL/MSFT and trades closer to peak-enthusiasm on the AI side than to early-buildup on the robotics side; freeing the slot for a cleaner robotics pure-play improves thematic precision.

news_evidence.summary: The Global X Robotics & AI ETF (BOTZ) just hit a 52-week high, with NVIDIA, Keyence, and Mitsubishi Electric as top holdings; 33% trailing-twelve-month return reflects AI-driven re-rating already largely realized.

BOTZ's NVIDIA-heavy top-line weighting means it behaves like a leveraged AI semis position rather than an independent robotics bet, reducing diversification value alongside the user's direct NVDA position.

rationale_overall: One-for-one swap inside the robotics bucket: BOTZ out, ROBO in. The net effect leaves the wave-bucket shape unchanged (3 AI singles, 1 each for robotics/rockets/nuclear/quantum/engineered-biology, 4 defensives) but sharpens the robotics representation as the humanoid wave moves from research demos into commercial pilots. This is consistent with the profile's instruction to invest early in next waves while they are still cheap relative to peak - ROBO's industrials-heavy mix is less correlated with the AI compute trade already saturated in BOTZ. No AI or defensive changes; AI is still mid-wave but not yet showing the peak-enthusiasm signals that would justify trimming individual names, and the defensive ballast (AGG, BIL, IAU, VIG) remains the appropriate counterweight.
